issues.title: Type file uses camelCase instead of PascalCase
issues.description: Type files should use PascalCase naming per convention (e.g. UserTypes.ts). The file categoryTypes.ts starts with a lowercase letter.

issues.title: Type file uses camelCase instead of PascalCase
issues.description: Type files should use PascalCase naming per convention (e.g. UserTypes.ts). The file galleryTypes.ts starts with a lowercase letter.

issues.title: Type file uses camelCase instead of PascalCase
issues.description: Type files should use PascalCase naming per convention (e.g. UserTypes.ts). The file paymentMethodTypes.ts starts with a lowercase letter.

issues.title: Type file uses camelCase instead of PascalCase
issues.description: Type files should use PascalCase naming per convention (e.g. UserTypes.ts). The file sectionItemTypes.ts starts with a lowercase letter.

issues.title: Utility file uses kebab-case instead of camelCase
issues.description: Utility files should use camelCase naming (e.g. formatDate.ts). The file export-csv.ts uses kebab-case.

issues.title: Boolean state variable missing 'is' prefix
issues.description: Boolean state variable 'open' should use is/has/can/should prefix per naming conventions. const [open, setOpen] = useState(false) should be const [isOpen, setIsOpen].

issues.title: Boolean state variable missing 'is' prefix
issues.description: Boolean state variable 'open' should use is/has/can/should prefix per naming conventions. const [open, setOpen] = useState(false) should be const [isOpen, setIsOpen].

issues.description: Boolean state variable 'open' should use is/has/can/should prefix per naming conventions. const [open, setOpen] = useState(false) should be const [isOpen, setIsOpen].

issues.title: Boolean state variable missing 'is' prefix
issues.description: Boolean state variable 'collapsed' should use is/has/can/should prefix per naming conventions. const [collapsed, setCollapsed] = useState(false) should be const [isCollapsed, setIsCollapsed].

issues.title: Boolean state variable missing 'is' prefix
issues.description: Boolean state variable 'collapsed' should use is/has/can/should prefix per naming conventions. const [collapsed, setCollapsed] = useState(false) should be const [isCollapsed, setIsCollapsed].

issues.title: Schema exported from types.ts instead of dedicated schema file
issues.description: The profileFormSchema Zod schema is defined in types.ts but the convention mentions schema.ts as a dedicated file for validation schemas. This is a minor concern since the naming convention lists schema.ts as a special file type.
issues.fix: Consider moving profileFormSchema to a separate schema.ts file in the same directory

patterns.pattern: Type files follow inconsistent naming convention
patterns.description: Most type files are correctly named types.ts but several feature-scoped type files use camelCase (categoryTypes.ts, galleryTypes.ts, paymentMethodTypes.ts, sectionItemTypes.ts). The convention mandates PascalCase for type files.
patterns.recommendation: Standardize all type-specific files to PascalCase as per the naming convention (UserTypes.ts pattern).

patterns.pattern: Boolean state variables often lack is/has prefix
patterns.description: Multiple components use 'open' and 'collapsed' as boolean state variable names instead of 'isOpen' and 'isCollapsed'. This pattern appears in 5 files across apps.
patterns.recommendation: Apply is/has prefix consistently to boolean state variables to improve readability and follow project conventions.

patterns.description: All component .tsx files follow PascalCase naming. Only Next.js reserved files (page.tsx, layout.tsx, error.tsx, providers.tsx) use lowercase, which is correct.
patterns.recommendation: No action required. This is a positive pattern.

patterns.pattern: Hook files consistently use use prefix
patterns.description: All hook files across the codebase correctly use the 'use' prefix (useAuth.ts, useProducts.ts, etc.). No violations found.
patterns.recommendation: No action required. This is a positive pattern.
